ADRO.name: Adaro Energy Indonesia
ADRO.summary: One of Indonesia's largest coal mining companies. Produces thermal coal for domestic and export markets.

AKRA.name: AKR Corporindo
AKRA.summary: Distributes fuel, chemicals, and industrial goods across Indonesia. Also develops industrial estates.

AMMN.name: Amman Mineral Internasional
AMMN.summary: Operates the Batu Hijau copper and gold mine in West Sumbawa. One of Indonesia's largest copper producers.

ANTM.name: Aneka Tambang
ANTM.summary: State-owned mining company producing gold, nickel, and bauxite. Operates the Pongkor gold mine.

ASII.name: Astra International
ASII.summary: Indonesia's largest conglomerate. Dominates automotive (Toyota/Daihatsu/Honda), heavy equipment, and financial services.

BBCA.name: Bank Central Asia
BBCA.summary: Indonesia's largest private bank by market cap. Known for retail banking, digital innovation, and strong credit quality.

BBNI.name: Bank Negara Indonesia
BBNI.summary: State-owned bank focusing on corporate and international banking. Strong in trade finance and treasury.

BBRI.name: Bank Rakyat Indonesia
BBRI.summary: Indonesia's largest bank by assets. Dominates microfinance through 7,000+ BRI Units across the archipelago.

BMRI.name: Bank Mandiri
BMRI.summary: Largest state-owned bank by assets. Strong in corporate banking, treasury, and wholesale transactions.

BRPT.name: Barito Pacific
BRPT.summary: Major petrochemical producer through subsidiary Chandra Asri. Also has plantation and property interests.

CPIN.name: Charoen Pokphand Indonesia
CPIN.summary: Indonesia's largest animal feed producer. Vertically integrated poultry business from feed to processed chicken.

ESSA.name: Essa Industries Indonesia
ESSA.summary: Operates a natural gas processing plant in West Java. Produces LPG and condensate from natural gas.

EXCL.name: XL Axiata
EXCL.summary: Major mobile network operator. Part of Malaysia's Axiata Group. Strong in data and digital services.

GOTO.name: GoTo Gojek Tokopedia
GOTO.summary: Indonesia's largest digital platform. Combines on-demand services (Gojek) and e-commerce (Tokopedia) with financial services.

HEAL.name: Medikaloka Hermina
HEAL.summary: Operates 40+ hospitals across Indonesia under the Hermina brand. Largest private hospital network by revenue.

ICBP.name: Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur
ICBP.summary: Consumer packaged goods arm of Indofood. Produces instant noodles (Indomie), dairy, snacks, and beverages.

INDF.name: Indofood Sukses Makmur
INDF.summary: Indonesia's largest food company. Produces Indomie instant noodles, owns plantations (Bogasari), and CPO operations.

INTP.name: Indocement Tunggal Prakarsa
INTP.summary: Second-largest cement producer in Indonesia. Part of HeidelbergCement Group. Operates 13 cement plants.

ITMG.name: Indo Tambangraya Megah
ITMG.summary: Coal mining company with operations in Kalimantan. Produces thermal coal for export markets.

KLBF.name: Kalbe Farma
KLBF.summary: Indonesia's largest pharmaceutical company by revenue. Produces prescription drugs, consumer health, and nutrition products.

MAPI.name: Mitra Adiperkasa
MAPI.summary: Indonesia's largest lifestyle retailer. Operates Sports Station, Sogo, Starbucks, Zara, and 3,000+ retail stores.

MDKA.name: Merdeka Copper Gold
MDKA.summary: Fast-growing mining company with gold, copper, and nickel operations. Owns Tujuh Bukit gold mine and nickel projects.

MIKA.name: Mitra Keluarga
MIKA.summary: Operates 16+ hospitals in major Indonesian cities under the Mitra Keluarga brand. Focuses on middle-class healthcare.

PGAS.name: Perusahaan Gas Negara
PGAS.summary: State-owned gas company. Distributes natural gas through 10,000+ km pipeline network across Indonesia.

PTBA.name: Bukit Asam
PTBA.summary: State-owned coal mining company. Operates the Tanjung Enim mine in South Sumatra. Produces thermal coal.

SIDO.name: Sido Muncul
SIDO.summary: Indonesia's largest herbal medicine manufacturer. Produces Tolak Angin, Kuku Bima, and other traditional remedies.

SMGR.name: Semen Indonesia
SMGR.summary: Indonesia's largest cement producer (state-owned). Operates under Semen Gresik, Semen Padang, and Semen Tonasa brands.

TLKM.name: Telkom Indonesia
TLKM.summary: Indonesia's largest telecom company. Owns Telkomsel (mobile), IndiHome (broadband), and extensive fiber infrastructure.

TPIA.name: Chandra Asri Pacific
TPIA.summary: Indonesia's largest integrated petrochemical company. Produces naphtha, ethylene, polyethylene, and polypropylene.

UNTR.name: United Tractors
UNTR.summary: Distributes Komatsu heavy equipment, operates coal mines, and runs mining contracting through Pamapersada Nusantara.
